summary:
Ganglionic transmission in autonic nervous system occurs when acetylcholine acts on postsynaptic nicotinic receptors found on postganglionic neurons. This activation leads to rapid depolarization through cation influx (e.g., Na+, Ca²+) resulting in neural excitability within autonomic ganglia.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$

The correct answer is D because ganglionic transmission involves acetylcholine binding to nicotinic receptors—specifically neuronal nicotinic receptors (nAChRs)—on postganglionic neuron membranes, triggering a depolarizing response essential for synaptic communication at autonomic ganglia. These mechanisms have been consistently supported across multiple scientific references without conflicting data regarding receptor type or neurotransmitter involvement.
